meta.title: Where docent lives
meta.subject: docent — placement on the explainer-tooling landscape

scenes.title: Where docent lives
scenes.footnote: freedom of expression × deterministic engine — and the trade we made

beats.narration: Every explainer tool sits somewhere on a two-dimensional plane. The horizontal axis is freedom of expression -- how much an author can shape the output. The vertical axis is engine determinism -- how reliably the same input produces the same output. This film places docent on that plane.
These two axes are not equivalent, and the bargain a tool strikes between them is the most load-bearing thing about it.

scenes.heading: The systems that already occupy this plane

novelty.statement: docent's author is an LLM constrained by a closed scene grammar; the human reviews placements, not pixels.

beats.narration: Three systems already occupy this terrain. Reveal.js, since two-thousand-eleven, is the slide deck as a web page -- a human writes every slide by hand. The deck is the artifact.
Manim, since two-thousand-fifteen, is a Python library for math animation. A human writes code; every render re-executes the program. Output is fragile across versions.
Screen recording is the floor: a human acts out the explanation on camera, and the take is whatever it was. No structure, no replay.
The line docent draws is on the authoring axis. The author is not a human; it is an LLM constrained by a closed scene grammar. The human reviews placements, not pixels. That is the new line.

scenes.heading: The trade-off plane — five tools, two axes

yAxis.label: Deterministic engine
yAxis.lowLabel: every render is a fresh program

beats.narration: Reveal.js sits center-bottom-right -- moderate freedom, very high reproducibility: the deck file plays the same way every time, but every slide is hand-written.
Manim sits in the bottom-right corner: maximum expressive freedom -- it is Python -- but minimum reproducibility, because every render re-executes a program against a moving library.
Excalidraw and screen recording both live on the right: high freedom, low determinism. A whiteboard sketch is whatever you drew that day; a screen recording is whatever the take was.

docent lives in the top-left, alone in its quadrant. A closed grammar of seventeen scene types -- so freedom is low; a deterministic Remotion engine -- so the same spec renders the same frames every time. That is the bargain.

scenes.heading: What we pay for the top-left corner

nodes.label: Reproducibility
nodes.sub: the same spec, the same film, on every machine

nodes.label: Lost expressivity
nodes.sub: no escape hatch — a film that wants free-form has nowhere to go

nodes.label: Grammar drift
nodes.sub: every new explanatory shape is a new scene type; the grammar grows

nodes.label: Closed allowlist
nodes.sub: every scene type ships its own validator; no free-form leaks in

beats.narration: The top-left corner is not free. We chose reproducibility over expressive freedom -- the same spec renders the same frames on every machine, but a film that wants a free-form shape has nowhere to go.

The risk we live with is grammar drift: every new explanatory shape is a new scene type, and the grammar grows. Today it is seventeen types; in a year it could be thirty. We rejected the alternative of a free-form scene type because once expressivity leaks in, reproducibility leaks out.

scenes.heading: Disposition — sound, with one watch-item
points: Disposition: sound. Reproducibility is worth the lost expressivity for the explainer mode.
Biggest residual risk: grammar drift — seventeen scene types today, more tomorrow.
Watch: how often a new film requires a new scene type. If the rate stays under one per quarter, the bargain holds.

beats.narration: Disposition: docent's placement is sound. Trading expressive freedom for engine determinism is the right bargain for an LLM-authored explainer.
The single biggest residual risk is grammar drift: every genuinely new explanatory shape currently requires a new scene type, and the grammar can balloon.
What to watch: the rate of new scene types per quarter. Under one is healthy; above two is the bargain quietly inverting -- the top-left corner becoming the top-right by accretion.
